title: Michael Gherrity
text: Home * People * Michael Gherrity Michael (Mike) Gherrity , an American computer scientist and AI-researcher from the University of California, San Diego . He defended his Ph.D. in 1993 - A Game Learning Machine , elaborating on SAL ( Search and Learn ) [1] , his General Game Playing program. While applying a move generator , and losing if own king is captured as sole domain specific knowledge, it was the first chess program used Temporal Difference Learning [2] . In a match of 4200 games against GNU Chess (One second per move), it started to play random moves within its two ply search plus Consistency Search , a generalized Quiescence Search [3] , but learned to play reasonable, but still weak chess. It archived eight draws, apparently due to a repetition detection bug in GNU Chess [4] .
